transcript.text: I'm good, how are you doing?
Good, sorry, I'm off camera because my neck is killing me, I'm laying down for a second.
Yeah, no worries, sorry to hear that, did you do something in particular?
I think I slept funny, I don't know.
I hate when that happens.

Yeah, I don't know, I'll be fine, it's just one of those things you just gotta rest it, and I just feel like I've been sitting in a chair all day and keeping my head up, and it's just making it worse.
Yeah, no, that's fair.
Is it just us today?
Or is Ali gonna hop in?
I don't I think Ali is gonna be here.
Okay, we'll a few minutes, worst case, you and I can chat over a couple things.
Thank you!
Thank
actually going to be a pretty quick meeting from my end, but I've got some deliverables in the works for you guys this week.
You know, we've been chatting a lot about Reddit, and I've been doing some more social research and digging in there.
And so I'm going to have some topic ideas to send over.
And in doing that, I'm also thinking through how we can combine that with editorial work that we're looking at.
Sounds good.
How's, gosh, how's your February been?
I can't believe it's, we're like halfway through it already.
Yeah, it is crazy.
The years just always go by so fast, it's nuts.
I know, he blanked it, it's not.
But I am, February is busy, I'd say.
I'd say that's the right term, it's just busy.
That's fair.
Yeah.
How about you?
Also busy, going really fast.
We got our first like actual winter storm here in Reno, and it's been.
It's snowing for the last three days.
No way.
It's like 80 degrees.
It's crazy.
It feels a little bit late in the season.
We don't normally get much, but I'm a Colorado gal at heart, so I always get really excited when we get a little bit of snow.
There you go.
Ali, how are you?
Doing great, Katya.
It's great to see you again.
Yeah, good to see you too.
I'm happy to connect this week.
I was just sharing with Alex that kind of a quick meeting.
I've been heads down in some strategy work and working on getting some stuff over to you guys by the end of the week, but wanted to connect just in case there was anything top of mind for you all.
A couple different things specifically that I wanted to chat about.
I know we had shared some documentation around some backlinking ideas and cost.
Wasn't sure if you guys had a chance to chew on that quite yet, had any questions, anything else that I could follow up on.
Um...
I don't think we have any questions.
Yeah, I think it's pretty straightforward.
I don't know if we'll expand necessarily to doing backlinking with you all, unless, Alex, you think otherwise.
But it was pretty helpful, so I appreciate you putting it together.
Yeah, no worries.
Wanted to make sure it was as much of a resource as information on what it would look like to go through with us.
Yeah, thank you.
Yeah.

One of the other things that I am working on as we kind of move towards building out that Reddit pipeline and starting to publish in March, I've been digging into some different social research around topic areas that would make sense to focus on.
So I'll share a cleaner version of this with some analysis by the end of the week is my goal.
But essentially what I've been doing is scraping, like looking through Reddit for different topics, a lot.

Lined with products and services that you guys offer based on the documentation that we've got right now, and thinking through different angles that we would want to take for those posts on that main page, and some potential positioning, and the answer targets here, essentially, part of Reddit is that it's an AI visibility play.

And so we want to be thinking about what kind of questions that we would be targeting in an answer, both questions people are asking about on Reddit, but those are also probably things that people are asking within AI as well.
So a couple different layers that I'm considering there, but wanted to give you guys a bit of a sneak preview.

And then one of the other things within this, because the Reddit topics are likely to be different than exactly how we would want to approach something on the blog, but I think there's probably some ideas we can uncover here around some editorial content that we can develop as well.
Sounds good.
And then I know we chatted quite a bit last week about like some goals.
tools around attribution and leads.
And I do have my technical team looking into your HubSpot.
So that's in progress.
And we'll share as soon as we've got some recommendations around how to get that set up and piped for you guys.
I just want to make sure you knew that hadn't fallen off our radar.
Cool.
Yeah, we are having a big company-wide push to adopt HubSpot more.
Okay.
So anything you can give us, ways that we can actually track the leads that are coming through on these efforts.
I just want to make sure our RevOps manager is aware of all this stuff.
Totally.
Yeah, we'll get some guidance over.

A lot of what we need for visibility that we look for is like a first touch attribution where essentially we're looking to see what URL folks came in to your site with and then, you know, where they went from there.
Sometimes it'll show like if they, however we want to track like what that lead looks like, whether it's, you know, clicking on the Calendly link and signing up.
Yeah.

Moving through that process, there's a few different layers that can be broken down, but typically the visibility that we look for in how that gets set up is like first touch URL, the date that they came in.
And we don't always need like any identifying information about the lead, but usually there's some sort of like just basic ID that tracks to make sure that it's unique.
So some different pieces there.

And then I also, I had flagged in a few of my messages because I know we've talked a little bit about some various like new services that you guys are offering in terms of like LinkedIn thought leadership messaging and those different elements.
And I want to make sure one of the pieces I'm working on with my team is building out like a really robust sort of product matrix that our pipeline can pull from.

So it doesn't just understand how you guys talk about different concepts, but also is really specifically plugging in the services that you're using and getting like more and more specific about that as we continue to generate content.
And.
Have with prospective clients and like some snippets that you'd want to call out for us to take a look at.
Those can also be super helpful.
We can scrape them and compile some documentation there as well.
Okay.
It's another way that we can also look at developing some more like content ideas too is thinking through that angle.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Cool.
And then I don't know if you saw, I flagged this in our chat, but I saw a kind of exciting visibility win in terms of like AI performance.
So looking at some of the prompts that we track for you all, our content showed up as one of the first citations in this AI overview.
And you guys also showed up in the response itself.
And so getting those two different wins is like really quite significant.
Specific call out here is that a lot of times within these AI overviews, they show up before even the first result within SEO.
And so what we're seeing more and more is that...
Like, it's very close to the headline text, so we can just make sure we're focusing on the spacing.
then, like, the AI tools for sales reps underneath, like, doesn't seem like it's consistent with how we're doing the other spacing of the text, you know what I mean?
Yeah, so there's, I can work with our designer to make sure that that buffer is established a little more clearly in our pipeline.
I think a lot of it has to do with the number of characters that fit per line, so I'll definitely align with them on that.
Okay, sounds good.

And the only other thing from my side is, I'm just looking at, like, the impression data in Search Console, and it looks like it's kind of, like, leveled off since a peak in around, like, end of January.
I don't know if that's expected, but I would love to continue to grow.
I was going to dig in there a little bit more, because I was seeing that as well.
I was curious if you guys...
Because I know you've been working on some site updates and things.
Because I did notice, yeah, January was high and then it looks like it dipped a little bit and held steady in February.
And we're seeing that across site-wide and to a slightly lesser degree on the blog.
And so I wasn't sure, like it could be, you know, site map updates or other changes that you guys might have made.
Curious if have any insight there.
Um, we, I don't think we've touched the main site.
I don't know about you, Alex.
No, I haven't.
The only thing that I've been doing is the landing pages.
Yeah, and those are on a subdomain, right?
Yeah, correct, yeah.
Cool, I'll dig in there a little bit more with my team as well.
That was with something that I was keeping my eye on this week too.
So I appreciate that you called that out for sure.
Awesome.
Cool, yeah, because, you know, just one of our goals is to continue the momentum.
And...
And grow as much as possible.
So I just want to make sure we're on track for that.
And if it's like an issue on our side, like we resolve it, it's something technical, you know?
Yeah, absolutely.
For sure.
Cool.
Anything else top of mind for y'all?
I think that was it for me.
Alex, I don't if there's anything on your side.
No, I guess a question that I had is what happens if I do start playing with our main website and making some edits to it?
Does it matter as long as I don't touch like the blog section or like, I guess, what's on and off limits?
Yeah, I would say the biggest thing, if you just give us a heads up about changes that you're going to make, because then we can have that in our mind as we're looking at performance.
I know there's so many different things that you can play with and adjust.
In terms of front-end design work, I don't anticipate that would make any drastic changes.
But if there's going to be things that impact, like linking that we might be doing to those pages, might be doing those pages or links.
or LinkedIn.
Thinking that you have within those pages to blog content or elsewhere, that can sometimes have an impact.
So thinking through some of those considerations can be helpful.
Outside of that, if there's changes that you're all making to the backend that might impact the sitemap or how Google crawls your site, that can definitely have a performance impact.
I'm not sure what the extent of some of the updates that you guys are looking to make.
Honestly, thinking about our homepage, I don't love.
Honestly, don't love the main website at all.
But there's some changes I want to make in terms of the services too.
So when you click into the services, I want to add LinkedIn thought leadership content on there.
And then I probably want to put kind of like the landing pages I just sent over to you, the content one, there's three of them there.
I don't want to put those exact landing pages because I don't want to publish our pricing publicly.
Got it.
I want to do something something very
Very similar in terms of the way the services are laid out and like what's included in the services on there.
Okay, that makes sense.
I don't anticipate that would do anything wild.
Out of curiosity, so these landing pages, you're sending these to folks like after sales calls?
Yeah, exactly.
Okay, but you wouldn't necessarily want us to link these in like in any blog content.
should still focus on the main services pages.
I Ali, what do you think?
I think we should have everything on one domain.

My whole issue is I don't want our competitors seeing our prices and honestly, we charge a lot and I don't want to scare people off before they book a call because we do have a tendency to sell really well.
So I don't want people to not book calls in the first place because they're intimidated by our pricing.
I'd rather them get on a call with us, be really, really into the idea of buying our services.
Figure out a way to get to secure budget to buy our services rather than be scared off and not even go through the process.
Does that make sense?
Those landing pages are already ranking organically in Google.
Yeah, they're included in all of our YouTube descriptions and stuff.
Yeah, but that's not where our calls are getting booked from.
They're getting booked from LinkedIn.
Yeah, I'm just saying, like, if you're worried about competitors finding it, the landing pages are already there.

Yeah, but what I'm saying is I also don't want people not booking with us because they saw us on LinkedIn, went to our website, and then they saw pricing and said, out of my budget, I'll never be able to afford that.
Not even worth booking.
Yeah.
So another layer, and I can – let me connect with my team because they might have some ideas in this realm as well.
Top of mind, in having content that is similar on this domain and your primary domain, it can actually create some – Right.
Yeah.
Competition for yourself, which might be part of what we're seeing in terms of like a dip in impressions, like dividing attention.
I know sometimes you can set up sitemaps so that certain pages don't index, so they're not going to be recognized by Google and put in front of people.
And that can be a way to avoid like driving a lot of traffic to a particular page, but still having it on your main domain.
So there might be some ways that we can balance out some of those concerns that we could recommend and look at as well.
Okay.
I'll tell you what, I'm also going to ask a peer group and see what they think about pricing, high ticket services.
Do you guys have your pricing on your website?
Do you know?
We have our starting pricing on our website.
Yeah.
Okay.
I don't think we have like all the different tiers and layers to it, but we do have our starting price.
Okay.
All right.
Let me get back to you on that one.
Okay.
Yeah.
If...
If you'd like us to dig in and give you some considerations, happy to do that as well.
Sounds good.
Awesome.
Anything else for you guys before we hop off?
That's it for my side.
Cool.
Oh, I did want to check in and I can also ping in the chat as well if there's any new YouTube content and transcripts and things.
But I know you guys aren't the two who actually give us that info.
So just wanted to flag that I think we're running through the last few that got updated.
I think there's a couple of new videos, right, Alex?
There's testimonial videos, yeah.
And there's two more that are about to get published as well.
Okay.
Do we want those on their own, like case study landing pages?
Could be cool.
Why not?
Yeah.
Yeah.
You have our YouTube link, right, Katya?
I do, yeah.
Yes, yeah.
So yeah, go for it.
No worries.
I was going to say as far as, because we had chatted about like those case study pages being different than like the podcast summary pages.
Yeah.
And I think the last time we talked, you guys were still thinking through if you wanted to potentially develop the case studies or if that was something that we could potentially support with.

So happy to look at like we would need to build a slightly different pipeline to tackle some of those topics and write that material since we'd be giving different instructions than the strict like podcast recaps that we do.
But if you want it styled in a similar way to the recaps, we could optimize that a little bit quicker.

I would say if you can just grab the transcripts and maybe just like create like articles based on the transcripts and we'll like call them case studies and maybe we can just edit the text a little bit from there.
But I don't know why we wouldn't do the same thing we're doing for the podcast for these testimonial videos because some of them are like eight minutes long.
So there's a lot of content, I think, to work with just to make a landing page for it.
Okay.
Yeah, I can do some experimenting with my team and see what we might be able to develop.
We'll probably need to do an alignment piece or two to make sure that it's kind of how you guys are thinking about it.
But something we can definitely look at incorporating.
Yeah, I think that would be cool.
Yeah, it might need a little bit more input, but at least we'll get the process started.
Yeah, for sure.
As far as priorities between doing that versus working on getting Reddit up and running, do you have a strong preference one way or the other?
I know you mentioned that you were starting to run some, you were looking at running some ads on Reddit.
So I want to make sure that if you're wanting to make sure that that page has content there that we're moving that needle forward to.
I mean, ideally both, I would say.
you.
Like, they're both priorities.
Yeah.
We could probably have read it up and running by, like, early March, and then by mid-March have some initial, like, test articles around the testimonials.
I guess that sounds fine.
I think for the testimonials, there wouldn't be much more than just the transcript.
So I don't know if, like, you're saying just, like, shipped on the blog itself?
That's how long it would take?
Yeah, because I think what we'll need to do, every slightly different version of an article that we do, we have to build some custom pipeline steps for it.
Because we, as we provide different inputs and prompt it in a slightly different way.
So we can test the testimonials in our podcast transcript pipeline.
But I think it's going to need some...
So
Refinement and customization to make sure that it actually comes out in kind of a case study format.
Yeah, I definitely agree with that.
Okay.
Yeah, so I just wanted to frame timing that like that turnaround, it's going to take a little bit longer than just like a week or two if we're also pushing through with Reddit.
All right, that makes sense.
Yeah.
Cool.
I'll get my team working on it.
Awesome.
Sounds good.
Great.
All right.
Well, that's everything that I had.
If anything else comes up, feel free to send me a ping.
Will do.
Appreciate it.
Have good one, guys.
Bye.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
You

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on marketing deliverables, performance, and new content initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance Dip:** Search impressions have leveled off since a late-Jan peak. GrowthX will investigate if this is a technical issue (e.g., sitemap) or self-competition from the subdomain.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
  - [**New Content:** Two new initiatives were approved: a Reddit content pipeline launching early March, and testimonial video summaries launching mid-March.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1229.0)
  - [**Pricing Strategy:** A debate on public pricing was tabled. GrowthX will research solutions (e.g., `noindex`) to balance lead generation with SEO consolidation.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=932.0)
  - [**HubSpot Tracking:** GrowthX is scoping HubSpot setup to enable first-touch attribution, a key requirement for Understory's company-wide adoption.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)

## Topics

### Search Performance & Pricing Strategy

  - [**Problem:** Search impressions have leveled off since a late-January peak.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
  - [**Hypothesis:** Self-competition from the subdomain may be dividing SEO attention.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1003.0)
  - [**Proposed Solution:** Consolidate services pages onto the main domain.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=928.0)
  - [**Blocker:** Alex opposes public pricing, fearing it will deter high-ticket leads before a sales call.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=932.0)
  - [**Resolution:** Alex will consult a peer group. GrowthX will research technical solutions (e.g., `noindex`) to hide pages from search while keeping them on the main domain.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1003.0)

### New Content Initiatives

  - [**Reddit Content Pipeline**](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
      - [**Goal:** Launch a Reddit content pipeline in early March.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Create AI-optimized posts targeting questions on Reddit and in AI overviews.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [**Deliverable:** Katya will provide topic ideas and analysis by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
  - [**Testimonial Video Summaries**](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1099.0)
      - [**Goal:** Convert testimonial videos into case study-style articles.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1144.0)
      - [**Method:** Adapt the existing podcast summary pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1144.0)
      - [**Timeline:** Launch mid-March, after the Reddit pipeline is live.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1229.0)

### HubSpot & Attribution

  - [**Goal:** Enable first-touch attribution in HubSpot to track lead sources.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)
  - [**Requirement:** Understory needs this setup to support a company-wide HubSpot adoption push.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)
  - [**Status:** GrowthX's technical team is scoping the setup and will provide recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)

### Other Updates

  - [**AI Visibility Win:** Understory content appeared as a first citation and in the response of an AI overview, a key win as these results often outrank traditional SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)
  - [**Backlinking:** Understory will not pursue backlinking services at this time.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
  - [**Product Matrix:** GrowthX is building a product matrix to improve content specificity. Understory can provide snippets of how they discuss services to aid this.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=443.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
      - [Deliver Reddit topic ideas and analysis by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
      - [Investigate the search impression dip and report findings.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
      - [Scope HubSpot attribution setup and provide recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)
      - [Research technical solutions for the pricing/domain consolidation issue.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1003.0)
      - [Adapt the content pipeline for testimonial video summaries (target: mid-March launch).](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1229.0)
  - [**Understory:**](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1052.0)
      - [Consult a peer group on public pricing for high-ticket services.](https://fathom.video/share/XTcAFVspxzWp1W6ssBzsxaCJ6ryWU7TX?tab=summary&timestamp=1052.0)

action_items.description: Send Reddit topics/analysis to Alex & Ali
Send HubSpot attribution guidance to Alex & Ali; cc RevOps
Align w/ designer on AI overview spacing; implement buffer
Investigate Search Console impressions dip; report to Alex & Ali
Consult peer group re: high-ticket pricing on site
Draft testimonial case study articles from YouTube transcripts; share w/ Alex & Ali
